moduleDescription:
This module implements an interface to Nim's runtime type information (RTTI). See the marshal module for an example of what this allows you to do.
Note: Even though Any and its operations hide the nasty low level details from its users, it remains inherently unsafe! Also, Nim's runtime type information will evolve and may eventually be deprecated. As an alternative approach to programmatically understanding and manipulating types, consider using the macros module to work with the types' AST representation at compile time. See for example the getTypeImpl proc. As an alternative approach to storing arbitrary types at runtime, consider using generics.

entries.name: AnyKind
entries.code:
AnyKind = enum
  akNone = 0,               ## invalid
  akBool = 1,               ## bool
  akChar = 2,               ## char
  akEnum = 14,              ## enum
  akArray = 16,             ## array
  akObject = 17,            ## object
  akTuple = 18,             ## tuple
  akSet = 19,               ## set
  akRange = 20,             ## range
  akPtr = 21,               ## ptr
  akRef = 22,               ## ref
  akSequence = 24,          ## sequence
  akProc = 25,              ## proc
  akPointer = 26,           ## pointer
  akString = 28,            ## string
  akCString = 29,           ## cstring
  akInt = 31,               ## int
  akInt8 = 32,              ## int8
  akInt16 = 33,             ## int16
  akInt32 = 34,             ## int32
  akInt64 = 35,             ## int64
  akFloat = 36,             ## float
  akFloat32 = 37,           ## float32
  akFloat64 = 38,           ## float64
  akFloat128 = 39,          ## float128
  akUInt = 40,              ## uint
  akUInt8 = 41,             ## uint8
  akUInt16 = 42,            ## uin16
  akUInt32 = 43,            ## uint32
  akUInt64 = 44              ## uint64
entries.description: The kind of Any.

entries.name: Any
entries.description:
A type that can represent any nim value.
Danger: The wrapped value can be modified with its wrapper! This means that Any keeps a non-traced pointer to its wrapped value and must not live longer than its wrapped value.

entries.name: toAny
entries.code: proc toAny[T](x: var T): Any {.inline.}
entries.description: Constructs an Any object from x. This captures x's address, so x can be modified with its Any wrapper! The caller needs to ensure that the wrapper does not live longer than x!

entries.name: kind
entries.code: proc kind(x: Any): AnyKind {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the type kind.

entries.name: size
entries.code: proc size(x: Any): int {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the size of x's type.

entries.name: baseTypeKind
entries.code: proc baseTypeKind(x: Any): AnyKind {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the base type's kind. If x has no base type, akNone is returned.

entries.name: baseTypeSize
entries.code: proc baseTypeSize(x: Any): int {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the size of x's base type. If x has no base type, 0 is returned.

entries.name: invokeNew
entries.code: proc invokeNew(x: Any) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Performs new(x). x needs to represent a ref.

entries.name: invokeNewSeq
entries.code: proc invokeNewSeq(x: Any; len: int) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Performs newSeq(x, len). x needs to represent a seq.

entries.name: extendSeq
entries.code: proc extendSeq(x: Any) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Performs setLen(x, x.len+1). x needs to represent a seq.

entries.name: setObjectRuntimeType
entries.code: proc setObjectRuntimeType(x: Any) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This needs to be called to set x's runtime object type field.

entries.code: proc `[]`(x: Any; i: int): Any {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Accessor for an any x that represents an array or a sequence.

entries.code: proc `[]=`(x: Any; i: int; y: Any) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Accessor for an any x that represents an array or a sequence.

entries.name: len
entries.code: proc len(x: Any): int {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: len for an any x that represents an array or a sequence.

entries.name: base
entries.code: proc base(x: Any): Any {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the base type of x (useful for inherited object types).

entries.name: isNil
entries.code: proc isNil(x: Any): bool {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: isNil for an x that represents a cstring, proc or some pointer type.

entries.name: getPointer
entries.code: proc getPointer(x: Any): pointer {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the pointer value out of x. x needs to be of kind akString, akCString, akProc, akRef, akPtr, akPointer or akSequence.

entries.name: setPointer
entries.code: proc setPointer(x: Any; y: pointer) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the pointer value of x. x needs to be of kind akString, akCString, akProc, akRef, akPtr, akPointer or akSequence.

entries.name: fields
entries.code:
iterator fields(x: Any): tuple[name: string, any: Any] {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over every active field of x. x needs to represent an object or a tuple.

entries.code:
proc `[]=`(x: Any; fieldName: string; value: Any) {.raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets a field of x. x needs to represent an object or a tuple.

entries.code:
proc `[]`(x: Any; fieldName: string): Any {.raises: [ValueError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets a field of x. x needs to represent an object or a tuple.

entries.code: proc `[]`(x: Any): Any {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Dereference operator for Any. x needs to represent a ptr or a ref.

entries.code: proc `[]=`(x, y: Any) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Dereference operator for Any. x needs to represent a ptr or a ref.

entries.name: getInt
entries.code: proc getInt(x: Any): int {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the int value out of x. x needs to represent an int.

entries.name: getInt8
entries.code: proc getInt8(x: Any): int8 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the int8 value out of x. x needs to represent an int8.

entries.name: getInt16
entries.code: proc getInt16(x: Any): int16 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the int16 value out of x. x needs to represent an int16.

entries.name: getInt32
entries.code: proc getInt32(x: Any): int32 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the int32 value out of x. x needs to represent an int32.

entries.name: getInt64
entries.code: proc getInt64(x: Any): int64 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the int64 value out of x. x needs to represent an int64.

entries.name: getBiggestInt
entries.code: proc getBiggestInt(x: Any): BiggestInt {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the integer value out of x. x needs to represent some integer, a bool, a char, an enum or a small enough bit set. The value might be sign-extended to BiggestInt.

entries.name: setBiggestInt
entries.code: proc setBiggestInt(x: Any; y: BiggestInt) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the integer value of x. x needs to represent some integer, a bool, a char, an enum or a small enough bit set.

entries.name: getUInt
entries.code: proc getUInt(x: Any): uint {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the uint value out of x. x needs to represent a uint.

entries.name: getUInt8
entries.code: proc getUInt8(x: Any): uint8 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the uint8 value out of x. x needs to represent a uint8.

entries.name: getUInt16
entries.code: proc getUInt16(x: Any): uint16 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the uint16 value out of x. x needs to represent a uint16.

entries.name: getUInt32
entries.code: proc getUInt32(x: Any): uint32 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the uint32 value out of x. x needs to represent a uint32.

entries.name: getUInt64
entries.code: proc getUInt64(x: Any): uint64 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the uint64 value out of x. x needs to represent a uint64.

entries.name: getBiggestUint
entries.code: proc getBiggestUint(x: Any): uint64 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the unsigned integer value out of x. x needs to represent an unsigned integer.

entries.name: setBiggestUint
entries.code: proc setBiggestUint(x: Any; y: uint64) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the unsigned integer value of x. x needs to represent an unsigned integer.

entries.name: getChar
entries.code: proc getChar(x: Any): char {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the char value out of x. x needs to represent a char.

entries.name: getBool
entries.code: proc getBool(x: Any): bool {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the bool value out of x. x needs to represent a bool.

entries.name: skipRange
entries.code: proc skipRange(x: Any): Any {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Skips the range information of x.

entries.name: getEnumOrdinal
entries.code:
proc getEnumOrdinal(x: Any; name: string): int {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the enum field ordinal from name. x needs to represent an enum but is only used to access the type information. In case of an error low(int) is returned.

entries.name: getEnumField
entries.code:
proc getEnumField(x: Any; ordinalValue: int): string {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the enum field name as a string. x needs to represent an enum but is only used to access the type information. The field name of ordinalValue is returned.

entries.name: getEnumField
entries.code: proc getEnumField(x: Any): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the enum field name as a string. x needs to represent an enum.

entries.name: getFloat
entries.code: proc getFloat(x: Any): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the float value out of x. x needs to represent a float.

entries.name: getFloat32
entries.code: proc getFloat32(x: Any): float32 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the float32 value out of x. x needs to represent a float32.

entries.name: getFloat64
entries.code: proc getFloat64(x: Any): float64 {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the float64 value out of x. x needs to represent a float64.

entries.name: getBiggestFloat
entries.code: proc getBiggestFloat(x: Any): BiggestFloat {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the float value out of x. x needs to represent some float. The value is extended to BiggestFloat.

entries.name: setBiggestFloat
entries.code:
proc setBiggestFloat(x: Any; y: BiggestFloat) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the float value of x. x needs to represent some float.

entries.name: getString
entries.code: proc getString(x: Any): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the string value out of x. x needs to represent a string.

entries.name: setString
entries.code: proc setString(x: Any; y: string) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the string value of x. x needs to represent a string.

entries.name: getCString
entries.code: proc getCString(x: Any): cstring {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the cstring value out of x. x needs to represent a cstring.

entries.name: assign
entries.code: proc assign(x, y: Any) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies the value of y to x. The assignment operator for Any does NOT do this; it performs a shallow copy instead!

entries.name: elements
entries.code: iterator elements(x: Any): int {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over every element of x. x needs to represent a set.

entries.name: inclSetElement
entries.code: proc inclSetElement(x: Any; elem: int) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Includes an element elem in x. x needs to represent a Nim bitset.
